English: Claim Sierra Leone

Now that we are independent, we should look for ways to expand our nation. by claiming the lands around the British colony of Freetown, we will be able to both gain new territory and open the possibility of purchasing Freetown.
A United Liberia
We have proven ourselves to be a united people, and despite many of our lands being gained by aggresive expansion or conquest, a majority of our citizens are glad to be apart of our glorius republic.
Claim Windward Coast
With Sierra Leone firmly under our control, we can now claim Windward Coast as well, even if it means disregarding the nations already governing the area.
Claim Ivory Coast
The next logical step in our expansion is The Ivory Coast. By claiming the lands for ourselves, we will be able to sieze new resources and lands, as well as offer to buy Grand Bassam from The French.
Proclaim Greater Liberia
With Liberia being as strong as ever, we now see that it is our mission to bring democracy and civilization to the lands of Western Africa.
Move Capital To Freetown
Many people in our government agree that Freetown would make an excellent capital for our Great Republic.
A Country Of Free Slaves
Founded in 1822 by the American Colonization Society (ACS), Liberia was to be a land for freed slaves to live in. We must continue this tradition by welcoming all freed slaves from the Americas.
Embrace The Fulbe
The Fulbe have liven in our republic for many years and have proven themselves to not be so different from us. We should grant them the same rights and privileges many of our other citizens enjoy.
Purchase Freetown
now that we have claimed Sierra Leone, we should offer to buy Freetown from the United Kingdom for a decent sum.
Purchase Grand Bassam
To complete our ownership of the Ivory Coast, we must purchase Grand Bassam from France.
Purchase The Gold Coast
Purchasing the Gold Coast is an excellent way to expand our nation and to have the local nations relinquish their claims on liberian land.
Purchase Bissau
Bissau will be an integral part of The Liberian Nation, though first we must purchase it from the Portuguese.
